article_title: Micron Revenue Triples to Record 23.9 Billion Dollars as AI Memory Supercycle Accelerates

content_preview.title: Micron Revenue Triples to Record 23.9 Billion Dollars as AI Memory Supercycle Accelerates
content_preview.summary: Micron Technology posted record fiscal Q2 revenue of 23.9 billion dollars, nearly tripling year over year on surging AI memory demand, while guiding fiscal Q3 to 33.5 billion dollars -- a single quarter that would exceed its entire annual revenue through fiscal 2024.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Micron Technology reported record fiscal second-quarter results on March 18, 2026, posting [revenue of 23.86 billion dollars](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/18/micron-mu-q2-earnings-report-2026.html) -- a 196 percent increase year over year and a 75 percent jump from the prior quarter. Non-GAAP earnings per share came in at 12.20 dollars, [beating the analyst consensus of 9.31 dollars](https://siliconangle.com/2026/03/18/microns-earnings-crush-forecasts-guides-even-bigger-beat-stock-f...

source_verification.https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/18/micron-m: CNBC page did not render article content (paywall). However, all financial details attributed to CNBC are independently confirmed by SiliconAngle.
source_verification.https://siliconangle.com/2026/03/18/micr: Fully confirmed: $23.86B revenue, $12.20 EPS vs $9.31 consensus, $13.78B net profit, 74% gross margin vs 37% prior year, Q3 guidance $33.5B/$19.15 EPS beating Street estimates of $24.3B/$12.05, Mehrotra quote about memory as strategic asset, stock gained 61% YTD and 351% over 12 months, capex rising meaningfully in FY2027.
source_verification.https://www.nasdaq.com/market-activity/s: Nasdaq stock data page — confirms Micron earnings data availability. Article's claim of muted after-hours reaction is consistent.
source_verification.https://www.tomshardware.com/tech-indust: Confirmed: Global chip sales $791.7B in 2025 (25.6% increase), SIA projects $1 trillion in 2026, driven by AI and elevated prices.

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All financial metrics verified through SiliconAngle: revenue, EPS, margins, guidance, and market context. SIA industry figures confirmed by Tom's Hardware. CNBC behind paywall but claims are corroborated.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Factual financial reporting with no sensationalism. Appropriately notes both the record results and the muted stock reaction. What to Watch section provides balanced forward-looking commentary.
editor_notes.originality: Comprehensive earnings coverage synthesizing financial results with HBM4 technology context and broader semiconductor industry trends.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Accurate financial news article with all key metrics verified. Strong earnings coverage with appropriate industry context. Approved.
